title: sorbus hupehensis var. aperta
intro: Sorbus hupehensis var. aperta, also known as Chinese rowan or Chinese whitebeam, is a deciduous shrub or small tree in the rose family (Rosaceae). The plant has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. Its health benefits include reducing fever, relieving pain, and improving respiratory and digestive health. In herbal preparations, the plant's bark, leaves, and roots are often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety as a medicinal agent.
